questions.questionText: Where was the black cat found?
questions.questionTextZh: 黑猫是在哪里被发现的？

options.label: A
options.text: At the gate of a grade school in Kent.

options.label: B
options.text: Under the engine cover of a man's car.

options.label: C
options.text: Inside the car of David King's neighbour.

options.label: D
options.text: Outside the office of a charity foundation.

questions.questionText: What do we learn about the cat at the end of the news report?
questions.questionTextZh: 新闻报道最后我们了解到关于这只猫的什么信息？

options.label: A
options.text: It got reunited with its owner.

options.label: B
options.text: It was injured during the rescue.

options.label: C
options.text: It was placed in the care of a UK charity.

options.label: D
options.text: It became a pet of Mr. King's grandson.

questions.questionText: What will Fayetteville witness in less than a month?
questions.questionTextZh: 不到一个月后费耶特维尔将见证什么？

options.label: A
options.text: The reunion of this year's Olympic gold medalists.

options.label: B
options.text: The opening of the Special Olympics Spring Games.

options.label: C
options.text: The first important political event in the next five years.

options.label: D
options.text: The 100th anniversary celebration of Methodist University.

questions.questionText: What did Benjamin Koalzac say is rewarding to see?
questions.questionTextZh: Benjamin Koalzac说什么是有回报的？

options.label: A
options.text: Volunteers visit the Games' website to sign up.

options.label: B
options.text: Children play in a fun tent and enjoy themselves fully.

options.label: C
options.text: Organizers devote their time and energy to the Games.

options.label: D
options.text: Athletes with special needs triumph in the Games.

questions.questionText: What has the German supermarket been ordered to do?
questions.questionTextZh: 德国超市被命令做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Compensate for the Swiss manufacturer's loss.

options.label: B
options.text: Change the wrapping of its commodities.

options.label: C
options.text: Destroy its imitation chocolate rabbits.

options.label: D
options.text: Defend itself in the country's commercial court.

questions.questionText: What did Switzerland's highest court suggest about the chocolate in question?
questions.questionTextZh: 瑞士最高法院对涉案巧克力有什么建议？

options.label: A
options.text: It could be reused in other products.

options.label: B
options.text: It could be resold cheaper to avoid waste.

options.label: C
options.text: It could be reshaped into animals other than rabbits.

options.label: D
options.text: It could be rewrapped and sold by the budget supermarket.

questions.questionText: Why did Switzerland's highest court overturn the commercial court's ruling?
questions.questionTextZh: 为什么瑞士最高法院推翻了商业法院的裁决？

options.label: A
options.text: To be fair to the German supermarkets.

options.label: B
options.text: To protect chocolate retailers' interests.

options.label: C
options.text: To prevent consumers' possible confusion.

options.label: D
options.text: To boost the growth of the chocolate industry.

questions.questionText: What does the man request the woman to do?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士请求女士做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Pass a book to him.

options.label: B
options.text: Make a vegetable dish.

options.label: C
options.text: Return the handbook to him.

options.label: D
options.text: Meet his sister's boyfriend.

questions.questionText: What does the woman say about eating vegetables only?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士对只吃蔬菜有什么看法？

options.label: A
options.text: It doesn't sound practical for her.

options.label: B
options.text: It would do harm to one's muscles.

options.label: C
options.text: It would reduce one's protein intake.

options.label: D
options.text: It doesn't seem to be a balanced diet.

questions.questionText: Why does the boyfriend of the man's sister choose to be a vegetarian?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士姐姐的男朋友为什么选择成为素食主义者？

options.label: A
options.text: To improve his health.

options.label: B
options.text: To protect animal rights.

options.label: C
options.text: To stick to his religious belief.

options.label: D
options.text: To follow a trendy lifestyle.

questions.questionText: On what point does the man agree with the woman at the end of the conversation?
questions.questionTextZh: 对话结束时男士在什么观点上同意女士？

options.label: A
options.text: The torture and distress dolphins suffer in parks.

options.label: B
options.text: The urgency of joining an animal rights group.

options.label: C
options.text: The gloomy environments animals are kept in.

options.label: D
options.text: The necessity of having zoos and eating meat.

questions.questionText: What part of the TV program does the man say was interesting?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士说电视节目的哪部分很有趣？

options.label: A
options.text: The part about not eating on board the plane.

options.label: B
options.text: The part about air travel in the past.

options.label: C
options.text: The part about getting over jetlag.

options.label: D
options.text: The part about avoiding sleeping on the plane.

questions.questionText: What does the man say he will do next time he flies a long distance?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士说他下次长途飞行时会做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Try following the advice given by the expert on the show.

options.label: B
options.text: Concentrate on reading articles recommended by experts.

options.label: C
options.text: Have meals as usual to stay away from hunger.

options.label: D
options.text: Take a nap to adjust to a new time zone.

questions.questionText: Why does the woman think she is lucky?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士为什么觉得自己很幸运？

options.label: A
options.text: She has been well treated when traveling.

options.label: B
options.text: She does not have to worry about jetlag.

options.label: C
options.text: She can spend a lot of time vacationing.

options.label: D
options.text: She does not have any problem flying.

questions.questionText: Why does the woman think the problem of being afraid to fly deserves attention?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士为什么认为害怕飞行的问题值得关注？

options.label: A
options.text: It affects twenty-five percent of people.

options.label: B
options.text: It has long been ignored by many experts.

options.label: C
options.text: It impacts female travelers more seriously.

options.label: D
options.text: It has caused heavy losses to many airlines.

questions.questionText: What does the speaker think is the best way to get started in user experience design?
questions.questionTextZh: 演讲者认为进入用户体验设计领域最好的方式是什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Learning from skilled designers.

options.label: B
options.text: Joining a 6-month training camp.

options.label: C
options.text: Obtaining real-world experience.

options.label: D
options.text: Anticipating actual challenges.

questions.questionText: What does the speaker say being effective requires?
questions.questionTextZh: 演讲者说有效工作需要什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Core skills.

options.label: B
options.text: Higher education.

options.label: C
options.text: Capability.

options.label: D
options.text: Adaptability.

questions.questionText: What can people do as long as they know their end goal and strive for it?
questions.questionTextZh: 只要人们知道自己的最终目标并为之努力，他们可以做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Face challenges.

options.label: B
options.text: Start anywhere.

options.label: C
options.text: Pursue roles in teams.

options.label: D
options.text: Follow their own path.

questions.questionText: Why did the school principal ask what needed to change?
questions.questionTextZh: 校长为什么问需要改变什么？

options.label: A
options.text: To allow students more freedom in their academic work.

options.label: B
options.text: To enable teachers to interact more with their students.

options.label: C
options.text: To engage students more in their learning.

options.label: D
options.text: To respond actively to students' requests.

questions.questionText: What has flexible seating meant at the speaker's school?
questions.questionTextZh: 灵活座位在演讲者的学校意味着什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Rearranging most traditional chairs and desks.

options.label: B
options.text: Adopting a variety of different seating options.

options.label: C
options.text: Shifting from traditional teaching to task-based learning.

options.label: D
options.text: Using new furniture to create a comfortable environment.

questions.questionText: What has flexible seating brought about at the speaker's school?
questions.questionTextZh: 灵活座位给演讲者的学校带来了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: A change to teachers' opinion of their students.

options.label: B
options.text: A harmonious relationship among its students.

options.label: C
options.text: A strengthened effect of its discipline.

options.label: D
options.text: A win for all people involved.

questions.questionText: Why were dozens of British students sent home on Tuesday?
questions.questionTextZh: 为什么周二几十名英国学生被送回家？

options.label: A
options.text: They arrived late for their first day of school.

options.label: B
options.text: They weren't informed of the school's updated rules.

options.label: C
options.text: They answered their headteacher's email in an impolite way.

options.label: D
options.text: They didn't wear the shoes required by the school's new policy.

questions.questionText: What can the price of school uniforms spark among families in financial difficulty?
questions.questionTextZh: 校服价格会在经济困难的家庭中引发什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Panic.

options.label: B
options.text: Anger.

options.label: C
options.text: Disputes.

options.label: D
options.text: Riots.

questions.questionText: What did the head teacher think of the school's new uniform rules?
questions.questionTextZh: 校长对学校的新校服规定有什么看法？

options.label: A
options.text: They rendered the school unique in the district.

options.label: B
options.text: They enhanced the positive image of the school.

options.label: C
options.text: They improved student behavior and performance.

options.label: D
options.text: They strengthened the school's discipline and order.

questions.questionText: What did Lucinda May have to do to buy her child the correct pair of shoes?
questions.questionTextZh: Lucinda May为了给孩子买正确的鞋子不得不做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Tighten her monthly budget.

options.label: B
options.text: Turn to her parents for help.

options.label: C
options.text: Borrow £65 from her friend.

options.label: D
options.text: Postpone paying her other bills.
